title: Israel Ministry of Foreign Affairs — Entry Visa to Israel Application Form
description: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs' (Global Consular Service) "Entry Visa to Israel - Application Form" (טופס בקשה למתן אשרת כניסה לישראל), the standard application submitted at an Israeli consular mission abroad by a foreign national seeking a visa to enter Israel. Opens Israel's Visa vertical (4 of 6). This document models the entire applicant-facing side of the form across its 3 pages — applicant identity, current travel document details, permanent address abroad, contact details, purpose and details of the intended visit, residence permit category, spouse and minor-children particulars, family/acquaintances in Israel, and the applicant's own signature block — but excludes the dotted-border "לשימוש משרדי" ("for office use") block at the foot of page 3 (visa grant number, visa type, validity period, single/multiple-entry designation, and consular remarks), which the applicant never fills in themselves. This document describes the form only; it does not submit anything on the applicant's behalf, and does not imply endorsement by the State of Israel or the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. GovSchema is independent and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or operated by the State of Israel.

authority.name: Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Global Consular Service)

source.documentRef: "Entry Visa to Israel - Application Form" (טופס בקשה למתן אשרת כניסה לישראל)

verification.notes:
GOV-3101 ("GovSchema Standard Research" cycle). Israel's Visa vertical was pre-scouted and left as disclosed backlog during the GOV-3094 cycle, which located this exact source URL and confirmed it a genuine, unauthenticated, native-text (non-scanned) PDF; this cycle independently re-fetched it from scratch via plain curl (no login/CAPTCHA gate on this direct embassies.gov.il media_upload URL) rather than trusting that prior note: HTTP 200, content-type application/pdf, content-length 375,167 bytes, sha256 3936353b36b102d6190496edc3b77a0c08a6cedcec1a7883c90cfe9cd2baf6bc. Extracted via pdfjs-dist (legacy CJS build, pinned to 2.16.105 for canvas-rendering compatibility): 3 pages, getFieldObjects() returning null and 0 page annotations on every page (a flat, non-fillable print-and-fill PDF, the same convention already established for this jurisdiction's other schemas). The text layer is native (not OCR/scanned), bilingual Hebrew (RTL) and English (LTR); as with il/moin/dr1-passport-application, the entire 3 pages were additionally rendered to 2.5x-scale PNGs via node-canvas and cross-checked against coordinate-sorted text dumps, since RTL extraction returns Hebrew text items in a visually jumbled order even after y/x-sorting.

The form prints several choices as two structurally-independent, mirrored checkbox pairs — one under a Hebrew label (read right-to-left) and one under the equivalent English label (read left-to-right) — for the same underlying option, rather than one checkbox per option. `documentType` (Passport / Laissez-Passer), `gender` (Male / Female), and `familyStatus` (Married / Single / Widowed / Divorced) each follow this pattern and are modelled as a single enum field per choice, following the identical convention this registry already established for il/moin/dr1-passport-application's own bilingual mirrored checkboxes.

`residencePermitCategory` required a coordinate-level pass (pdfjs-dist getTextContent() item x/y positions, not just line-joined text, since the two-column-by-three-row Hebrew grid and its English gloss column are offset by roughly one row) to resolve unambiguously: the Hebrew grid's right column reads (top-to-bottom) "ביקור", "ארעי", "עבודה" at x≈262-290pt, and its left column reads "תושב", "עולה" at x≈207-231pt (rows confirmed by shared y-coordinates, e.g. row 1 Hebrew at y=394.7); the English gloss column prints "Visitor"/"Resident" on row 1 (y=394.4, matching the Hebrew row above), a single combined phrase "Temporary Resident" on row 2 (y=379.7, glossing the row's own "ארעי"), and "Immigrant"/"Work" on row 3 (y=365.2 — "Immigrant" glosses row 2's own "עולה", offset down by one display row from its Hebrew counterpart, while "Work" aligns with row 3's own "עבודה"). This yields 5 distinct categories — Visitor, Resident, Temporary Resident, Immigrant, Work — consistent with Israel's own real-world entry/residence-status taxonomy for a consular entry-visa form; modelled as a single required enum field (VISITOR, RESIDENT, TEMPORARY_RESIDENT, IMMIGRANT, WORK) rather than guessed from line-joined text alone.

The source form carries no asterisks, bolding, or any other explicit required/optional marker anywhere on any of its 3 pages (confirmed by a full-text scan for '*', 'חובה', and 'חייב', all absent) — unlike il/tax-authority/1301-personal-details or il/moin/dr1-passport-application, which had other structural cues. In the absence of any such marker, core applicant identity, current travel-document, permanent-address-abroad, contact-purpose, and visit-detail fields are modelled as required, while fields describing family members, additional contacts, or a second phone/email box are modelled as optional; this judgment call is disclosed here rather than left implicit. The source form prints two identically-labelled "מס' טלפון נייד" / "Mobile No." columns and two identically-labelled "E-mail" / "כתובת אלקטרונית" boxes, each pair with no distinguishing sub-label anywhere on the page (confirmed via coordinate dump, not assumed); both are modelled as two generically-ordered optional fields (mobilePhoneNumber1/2, emailAddress1/2) in the form's own left-to-right column order, rather than invented a distinguishing purpose the form itself does not state.

`returnVisaDetails` is modelled as requiredWhen `documentType` equals `LAISSEZ_PASSER`, following the form's own instruction directing this question specifically at applicants travelling on a laissez-passer issued by their state of permanent residence.

The "ילדים עד גיל 18" (Children under the Age of 18) table prints a fixed 6-row grid (given name, place of birth, date of birth), and the "קרובים / מכרים בישראל" (Family/Acquaintances in Israel) table prints a fixed 4-row grid (name, relation, address); both are closed, bounded tables rather than open-ended repeating structures, and GovSchema's repeating-groups proposal (GSP-0009) is not yet part of the accepted v0.3 specification, so each is modelled as its own bounded per-row field set (child1..child6, contact1..contact4), following the bounded-repeating-group convention already established elsewhere in this registry (e.g. entrantN/childN patterns in other jurisdictions' multi-dependent forms). All spouse, child, and contact fields are optional since they apply only when the applicant has a dependent or contact to declare.

Scope was cut at the form's own dotted-border box at the foot of page 3, headed "לשימוש משרדי" ("for office use"): everything above it (all applicant-facing sections through the applicant's own signature block) is modelled; the visa-grant number, visa type, validity period and duration, single/multiple-entry designation, and consular remarks inside that box are consular-office-only processing data the applicant never supplies, and are out of scope — the same convention this registry applies to other jurisdictions' clerk-only intake/approval sections (including this jurisdiction's own DR/1 and Form 1301 schemas). The 3 paragraphs of the page-3 "הצהרה" (Declaration) section — accuracy of the submitted details and absence of any criminal offence/threat/illness (paragraph 1), absence of any expulsion order or prior entry refusal (paragraph 2), and acknowledgement that a granted visa does not override the Ministry of Interior's own authority to deny entry if the visa was obtained on false information (paragraph 3) — are each modelled as a required `documents[]` attestation entry carrying the form's own quoted declaration text, per this registry's established convention for signed/quoted declaration blocks, rather than encoding the statements as fields. The signature block's own re-printed "שם המבקש" (Applicant's Name) blank, distinct from the familyName/givenName fields captured on page 1, is modelled as `applicantNameAtSignature`; the signature itself is not modelled as a field (the 3 declaration attestations above already carry the substance of what is being attested to).

2 valid mock scenarios (a passport-holding tourist applicant travelling alone with no dependents or additional contacts declared; a laissez-passer-holding applicant who states their return-visa details, with a spouse, 2 children, and 2 Israel-based contacts declared) plus 5 mutation-control fixtures (a missing statically-required field, a missing returnVisaDetails field while documentType is LAISSEZ_PASSER, a missing required declaration attestation, an invalid documentType enum value, and an invalid date format) are committed under `conformance/il/mfa/entry-visa-application/1.0.0/`. An ephemeral, from-scratch conformance checker (deriving required/requiredWhen rules directly from this schema's own fields[]/documents[], discarded after use, not committed) ran all 7: both valid scenarios at 0 errors, all 5 mutation controls each raising exactly 1 error. The same script confirmed the one requiredWhen field reference (documentType) resolves. Validated clean with node tools/validate.mjs and node tools/validate-ajv.mjs, individually and as part of the full registry run. Israel now stands at 4 of 6 verticals; National ID and DMV remain confirmed weak/screened dead ends from the GOV-3078 cycle (MR41a's own /AcroForm/Fields array is empty; the DMV service-index page 403'd).

fields.name: documentType
fields.label: דרכון / תעודת מעבר
fields.description: Whether the travel document the applicant holds and will travel on is a national passport or a laissez-passer. The form prints this choice as two mirrored checkbox pairs (one Hebrew-labelled, one English-labelled) for the same two options; both mirrors are modelled as a single field, following this registry's established convention for this jurisdiction's bilingual mirrored checkboxes (see il/moin/dr1-passport-application).
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, third table, left block — "דרכון" / "Passport" and "תעודת מעבר" / "Laissez Passer"

fields.sourceRef: Page 1, note below the document table — "אם ברשותך תעודת מעבר שהוצאה על ידי מדינת ישיבתך הקבועה... אנא ציין אם הוענקה לך אשרת חזרה ומה תוקפה"

fields.name: expectedEntryDateAndPlaceInIsrael
fields.label: תאריך ומקום הכניסה המשוער לישראל
fields.description: The applicant's expected date and place (border crossing/airport) of entry into Israel. The form prints date and place as a single combined column with no internal sub-division, modelled here as one free-text field.

fields.name: transitCountries
fields.label: ארצות מעבר

fields.sourceRef: Page 2, entry-details table — "ארצות מעבר" / "Countries of Transit"

fields.name: previousStaysInIsrael
fields.label: תאריכי שהיות קודמות בישראל
fields.description: Dates of the applicant's previous stays in Israel, if any. The form prints this as a three-blank-column area with no internal sub-headers; modelled as a single free-text field.
fields.sourceRef: Page 2 — "תאריכי שהיות קודמות בישראל" / "Dates of Previous Visits to Israel"

fields.name: residencePermitCategory
fields.label: סוג רישיון ישיבה
fields.description: The category of residence permit or entry status requested. The form prints five categories across a two-column, three-row checkbox grid, each mirrored in a Hebrew-labelled and an English-labelled checkbox (see documentType for the same mirrored-checkbox convention); the two rightmost checkboxes in the top row correspond to "ביקור"/"Visitor", the two leftmost in the top row to "תושב"/"Resident", the second row's right checkbox to "ארעי" (glossed "Temporary Resident") and left checkbox to "עולה"/"Immigrant", and the third row's single checkbox to "עבודה"/"Work".
fields.sourceRef: Page 2 — "סוג רישיון ישיבה" / "Residence Permit Category" checkbox grid

documents.label: הצהרת נכונות הפרטים והיעדר עבירה
documents.statement: אני מצהיר/ה שהפרטים שנמסרו לעיל נכונים ונמסרו בהכרה שהם מהווים יסוד לדיון בבקשתי. כמו כן, אני מצהיר בזה שלא עברתי עבירה פלילית. לא פעלתי נגד העם היהודי וביטחון מדינת ישראל. לא חליתי במחלה העלולה לסכן את בריאות הציבור. לא קיים נגדי צו מעצר שיפוטי ואינני מבוקש על ידי המשטרה של מדינה כלשהי.

documents.label: הצהרה על היעדר צו הרחקה או סירוב כניסה
documents.statement: אני מצהיר/ה בזאת שלא קיים נגדי צו הרחקה ולא סירוב כניסה לארץ, ידוע לי שאם יש נגדי מניעה כאמור, תימנע ממני אפשרות להיכנס לישראל ואוחזר למדינה שממנה הגעתי.

documents.label: הכרה בכך שהאשרה אינה שוללת את סמכות משרד הפנים
documents.statement: ידוע לי שאין בקבלת האשרה משום ביטול סמכות משרד הפנים בישראל, לסרב כניסתי לתחומי מדינת ישראל, אם יסתבר שהאשרה התקבלה על סמך פרטים כוזבים.
